_meta.description: BMFFFL notable trade history (2020-2025). Covers the 3 best and 3 worst trades in league history plus per-owner trade summaries. Full transaction log available via Sleeper API. Total trade volume: 257 trades across 6 seasons.

tradeVolumeByseason.notes: Inaugural Sleeper season — high activity as owners positioned rosters
Championship season; new owners (Tubes94, MCSchools) shifted dynamics
Steady market; Escuelas (new owner) generated early activity
Most active season; multiple build/teardown cycles converged
Dominated by Grandes fire sale + MLSchools12 win-now moves

notableTrades.summary: Tubes94 acquired the hottest dynasty WR in football for a 1st round pick that — due to Cogdeill11's subsequent standings improvement — landed around pick 9-10, not near the top. Cogdeill11 sold a breakout asset before confirming the breakout.
notableTrades.impact: Direct catalyst for Tubes94's transformation from 4-10 (2022) to runner-up (2024-2025). The Puka Nacua acquisition is the single most consequential trade in the Tubes94 dynasty arc.
notableTrades.teamRenaming: Tubes94 renamed their team 'Nacua Matata' for 2024 season.

notableTrades.summary: Three 2021 first-round picks, a second-round pick, and Le'Veon Bell for the consensus #1 dynasty player in the format. All of eldridsm's picks came from the best team in the league — meaning they were late in each round, not near the top. Le'Veon Bell was already washed.
notableTrades.impact: CMC remained the #1 dynasty RB for 5 more years. MLSchools12 went 11-3 in 2021 and won the championship. Foundational to their dynasty dominance.
notableTrades.historicalNote: McCaffrey would win a Super Bowl in this window. The three late firsts combined were not worth his dynasty value.

notableTrades.summary: Grandes acquired the best QB in dynasty (Mahomes, age 24, coming off MVP season) plus Gesicki for Goff, an unproven rookie Taylor, and a single 1st round pick. mmoodie12 valued uncertain futures over the best player at the most valuable position in SF.
notableTrades.impact: Grandes went 10-4 in 2021 and won the 2022 championship. Mahomes was the engine. mmoodie12 left the league after 2020.
notableTrades.historicalNote: Jonathan Taylor became excellent — but the dynasty arithmetic still favored Grandes, who got immediate, certain value in the best SF asset.

notableTrades.summary: The Nacua sale from Cogdeill11's perspective. Sold the hottest dynasty asset in football for a single first-round pick that didn't materialize as a top pick. The defining mistake of Cogdeill11's dynasty arc.
notableTrades.impact: Cogdeill11 has not made the playoffs since 2021. The Nacua sale directly accelerated their decline window.

notableTrades.summary: tdtd19844 paid peak price for Taylor coming off his monster 2021 season (1,811 rush yards, 18 TDs), then went 3-11 in 2022 — the worst record in the league. Taylor's Colts situation deteriorated. Tubes94 received three picks from a 3-11 team, which were near the top of each round — and used that capital to rebuild.
notableTrades.impact: This is the trade that launched Tubes94's rebuild. Three near-top picks from the worst team is exceptional return. They used those picks to build toward the 2023 Nacua trade and eventual 2024-2025 championship contention.
notableTrades.historicalNote: Tubes94 renamed their team 'Burn it all' immediately after this era of selling, signaling an intentional teardown.

notableTrades.summary: MLSchools12 overpaid in win-now mode. Gave two established players plus a 1st-round pick for Bucky Irving. They went 13-1 — dominant — but lost in the semis again. The ring wasn't won. Tyler Warren continued developing as a top TE for Cmaleski.
notableTrades.impact: MLSchools12's 2026 1st round pick (from a team that finished 3rd/13-1) is approximately pick 10-11. Still, the cost of two players plus that pick for Bucky Irving without winning a championship is the most costly non-winning trade in the archive.
notableTrades.historicalNote: This is MLSchools12's consistent pattern: surgical win-now acquisitions that almost work but don't produce the ring. The Bucky Irving trade is the 2025 version of that pattern.

ownerTradeProfiles.bestTrade: CMC acquisition 2020 — paid appropriately for the best asset, used it to win 2021 and sustain a 6-year dynasty.
ownerTradeProfiles.worstTrade: Selling DK Metcalf to Escuelas for Cam Akers + picks (2022). Akers was a bust. Metcalf kept producing.
ownerTradeProfiles.pattern: Wins when buying elite players. Loses when moving veterans for depth. The 2025 Bucky Irving overpay fits the established pattern.

ownerTradeProfiles.bestTrade: Puka Nacua acquisition 2023 + selling Jonathan Taylor at peak 2022.
ownerTradeProfiles.worstTrade: None notable — their trade record is the best of any owner in the Sleeper era.
ownerTradeProfiles.pattern: Sell veterans at peak. Buy rising assets. Accumulate picks during rebuild. Execute the dynasty playbook correctly.
ownerTradeProfiles.note: Best trade record despite 0 championships — all the building blocks are in place.

ownerTradeProfiles.overallGrade: Positive on major trades, mixed on volume
ownerTradeProfiles.bestTrade: Mahomes acquisition 2020 + 2025 fire sale (sold Barkley for two first-round picks).
ownerTradeProfiles.worstTrade: 2020 acquisition spree of aging veterans (Fitzgerald, Green, etc.) for picks that could have built future rosters.
ownerTradeProfiles.pattern: Oscillates between aggressive acquisition phases and sell-everything teardowns. The 2022 championship validated one cycle. The current rebuild is in early stages.

ownerTradeProfiles.bestTrade: D'Andre Swift acquisition from Escuelas (Week 11, 2023) — bought a capable RB with a near-top-3 1st, used it during their 2023 championship run.
ownerTradeProfiles.worstTrade: 2024-2025 fire sale — sold all future picks to Grandes/MLSchools12 for present assets, leaving zero 2026 picks.
ownerTradeProfiles.pattern: Best trades are surgical championship-window acquisitions. They understand when to push. The 2024-2025 fire sale was the logical conclusion of a championship-window mentality.

ownerTradeProfiles.bestTrade: Multiple 2022-2023 acquistions that powered playoff runs.
ownerTradeProfiles.worstTrade: Selling CMC for Bell + 3 late firsts (2020). The late firsts (from the best team in the league) were not worth the consensus #1 dynasty player.
ownerTradeProfiles.pattern: Sold prime assets at the wrong price in 2020. Trade activity tapered off completely by 2024-2025. No major acquisitions since 2023.

ownerTradeProfiles.bestTrade: 2020-2021 acquisitions that built their championship team.
ownerTradeProfiles.worstTrade: Selling Puka Nacua before confirming the breakout (2023) — the single most defining bad trade in BMFFFL history.
ownerTradeProfiles.pattern: Active trade history but consistently not capturing full value — either selling too early or buying fringe players.

ownerTradeProfiles.bestTrade: De'Von Achane acquisition 2024 — got a dynamic RB for a late 1st from their own 11-3 season.
ownerTradeProfiles.pattern: Tends to buy at the right moments. Solid acquisition history with no major overpays identified.

ownerTradeProfiles.bestTrade: Derrick Henry acquisition 2021 for 1st + 2nd — Henry delivered another elite season, powered runner-up run.
ownerTradeProfiles.worstTrade: Conservative hold approach may have cost upside opportunities.
ownerTradeProfiles.pattern: Relatively thin trade history — holds and lets the draft work. Conservative approach may explain 0 championships despite two runner-up appearances.

leagueWidePatterns: Selling at peak value consistently outperforms buying at peak (except when buyer has clear championship window)
Escuelas was systematically undervalued as a trading partner 2022-2024 — their picks had top-3 value in each round
MLSchools12's championship trades were worth it; their other trades were not
Trade volume correlates with active rebuilds, not performance
The best draft capital comes from tanking teams — Escuelas' picks were near the top of each round
